name: Watchmaker, Los Serrano Path and Fuensanta Sanctuary — gravel return through El Valle Natural Park
name_original: Relojero, Camino de Los Serrano y Santuario de la Fuensanta — vuelta en gravel por el Parque Natural de El Valle
region_intro: Touring cycling around Rio Segura offers a diverse environment characterized by riverine landscapes, agricultural areas, and varied terrain. The region features numerous greenways and trails that follow the Segura River, providing views of orchards and historic sites. Beyond the riverbanks, the landscape includes hills and canyons, with some routes traversing rocky mountain trails. This area provides a mix of paved and unpaved segments suitable for different fitness levels. Best touring cycling routes around Rio Segura The most popular touring cycling route is Segura River Meanders and Vía Amable 1 Trail, a 14.8 miles (23.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 27 minutes to complete. This easy route features mostly paved surfaces and minimal elevation gain, ideal for families. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Segura River Greenway, an easy 17.4 miles (28.0 km) path. This route offers flat terrain and views of agricultural landscapes along the river. Local touring cyclists also love the Segura River Trail, a 19.5 miles (31.4 km) trail leading through orchards and by the historic La Contraparada dam, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Touring cycling around Rio Segura is defined by riverine greenways, extensive agricultural areas, and diverse terrain ranging from flat paths to more challenging hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family-friendly rides to difficult routes. The routes in Rio Segura are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 9,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Rio Segura's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Rio Segura region?
A: Based on komoot data, there are over 470 touring cycling routes in the Rio Segura region, catering to various skill levels, from easy to difficult.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Rio Segura?
A: Yes, the Rio Segura region offers several easy routes suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017172684/segura-river-meanders-and-via-amable-1-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segura River Meanders and Vía Amable 1 Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14.8 miles long with mostly paved surfaces and minimal elevation gain. Many routes following the Segura River are generally flat and accessible.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Rio Segura?
A: The Rio Segura region offers diverse terrain. You'll find many routes along the river that are flat and mostly paved, passing through agricultural areas and orchards. However, some routes venture into hills and canyons, featuring unpaved sections, rocky mountain trails, and dedicated cycle lanes, requiring good to very good fitness.

Q: What are some easy touring cycling routes for beginners in Rio Segura?
A: For beginners, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016669718/segura-river-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segura River Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 17.4-mile route offers flat terrain and scenic views of agricultural landscapes. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016693916/segura-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segura River Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 19.5 miles long and leads through orchards and past the historic La Contraparada dam.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region has more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013500984/rio-segura-trail-gr-127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Segura Trail (GR 127)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 17.7-mile route in Valle de Ricote, featuring diverse terrain including rocky mountain trails. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017204133/watchmaker-los-serrano-path-and-fuensanta-sanctuary-gravel-return-through-el-valle-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Watchmaker, Los Serrano Path and Fuensanta Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; route, which includes a gravel return through El Valle Natural Park with significant elevation gain.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The Rio Segura region is rich in natural and historical sights. You can explore riverine landscapes, orchards, and historic sites like the La Contraparada dam. Some routes pass by reservoirs such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santomera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1553819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mayés Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features unique geological formations like the Barrancos de Gebas, known for its 'badlands' scenery, and the Almadenes Canyon formed by the Segura River.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Rio Segura?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017145041/santomera-reservoir-and-monteagudo-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santomera Reservoir and Monteagudo Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 19.3-mile ride. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33760168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Contraparada Park Bridge – La Contraparada of the Segura River loop from Murcia del Carmen&lt;/a&gt;, a longer moderate route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Rio Segura?
A: The Rio Segura region generally enjoys a Mediterranean climate. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are often considered the best times for cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather than the hot summer months. Winter can also be mild and suitable for cycling.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the Rio Segura region?
A: The Rio Segura region is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 700 reviews. Touring cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from flat river paths to more challenging hills, and the scenic views of agricultural landscapes and historic sites along the Segura River.

Q: Can I spot wildlife while cycling in Rio Segura?
A: Yes, the region offers opportunities for wildlife spotting, especially in natural areas. The Segura River originates in the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, which is home to golden eagles, griffon vultures, mountain goats, red deer, and wild boar. Along the riverbanks, particularly in areas like Almadenes Canyon, you might spot wildlife such as eagle owls and otters.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by significant historical sites?
A: Absolutely. Many routes follow the Segura River, allowing you to encounter historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016693916/segura-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segura River Trail&lt;/a&gt; passes by the historic La Contraparada dam. Further afield, the region features charming villages, castles, and religious sites like the Sanctuary of the Virgin of Hope (located in a cave system) and Murcia Cathedral.
tips: At this point we can decide to climb the Pico del Relojero, located at an altitude of 604 meters above sea level, thus offering us magnificent views of the entire environment. You can climb gravel without many problems.

Good variant to avoid technique and difficult Senda Bonita where we would surely have to put our feet on the ground if we rode gravel. On this path we will not have any problems and the views are also magnificent.

Sanctuary of the Virgen de la Fuensanta, patron saint of Murcia. Popular area of passage for bicycle lovers. The Sanctuary is located 5.5 km from the city of Murcia and surrounded by nature with magnificent views of the Huerta del Segura.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of the Virgin of La Fuensanta
